message:
feat: self-update check, subscription-based TUI ticks, atomic installer, and doctor enhancements

CLI:
- Add `self-update` subcommand with `--check` flag that queries GitHub
  releases API for newer versions, with 24h result caching
- Extend `doctor check` with binary_resolution probe (detects alias
  shadowing across zsh/bash login shells) and timestamp_format probe
  (warns if DB needs migration)
- Update `migrate` subcommand help to show new --check, --force, and
  --backup-dir options

TUI:
- Replace hand-rolled idle tick cadence (idle_tick_streak, Predictive
  strategy) with ftui_runtime Subscription-based housekeeping tick and
  ScreenTickDispatch trait, simplifying tick scheduling and removing
  ~60 lines of adaptive tick machinery
- Add HousekeepingTick message variant and subscriptions() impl
- All screens now tick uniformly via ScreenTickDispatch instead of
  per-screen gated ticking through the predictive strategy

Installer:
- Add atomic_install() for crash-safe binary writes (tmp + sync + mv)
- Add migrate_env_config() to convert Python .env to Rust config.env,
  stripping aiosqlite prefixes and Python-only vars
- Add interactive Python migration prompt in non-easy mode
- Add verify_installation() post-install checks (binary presence,
  version output, login-shell PATH resolution, alias displacement)

Tests & fixtures:
- Update cli_help fixtures for migrate options and self-update command
- Add binary_resolution and timestamp_format entries to doctor_check
  fixture
- Replace idle-cadence tests with ScreenTickDispatch coverage

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-28mgh.1.5","title":"T1.5: Stop running Python server processes","description":"Gracefully stop any running Python mcp_agent_mail server processes:
1. Find processes: pgrep -f "mcp_agent_mail" or "uvicorn.*mcp_agent_mail"
2. Send SIGTERM first, wait up to 5 seconds
3. If still running, send SIGKILL
4. Verify process is stopped
5. If the HTTP port (default 8765) is still occupied, warn the user

Do NOT force-kill unless the user passed --force or the installer is in non-interactive mode.
In interactive mode, prompt: "Python mcp_agent_mail server is running (PID 12345). Stop it? [Y/n]"

 {"id":"br-28mgh.2","title":"T2: Installer Hardening","description":"Track 2: Installer Hardening

The current install.sh has several usability gaps that prevent a seamless first-run experience.
This track addresses all of them to achieve world-class reliability.

## Scope
- Make --easy-mode the DEFAULT for pipe installs (detect stdin is not a tty)
- Ensure PATH is correct and takes precedence
- Add post-install verification (am --version must return Rust version)
- Handle shell config conflicts (alias vs binary precedence)
- Add --migrate flag (default: auto-detect, offer migration)
- Improve error messages with actionable remediation steps
- Add --uninstall flag for clean removal
- Support Windows (PowerShell install script)
- Atomic binary replacement (write to .tmp, rename)
- Handle concurrent installs properly (lockfile)

## Key Design Decision
When install.sh detects it is being piped (stdin is not a tty), it should automatically
enable --easy-mode behavior because the user cannot be prompted interactively.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:30.981599918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:30.981599918Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["track"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.2","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:30.981599918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.2.1","title":"T2.1: Auto-enable easy-mode for pipe installs","description":"When install.sh is piped (curl | bash), the user cannot interact with prompts.
Currently --easy-mode must be explicitly passed. Fix:

1. At the top of install.sh, detect pipe: if [ \! -t 0 ]; then EASY=1; fi
2. This auto-enables PATH updates in shell rc files
3. Also auto-enables non-interactive mode for any prompts
4. The --easy-mode flag still works explicitly for direct invocation
5. Add --no-easy flag to opt out even in pipe mode

This is the #1 usability fix -- without it, pipe install does not modify PATH,
so the user must manually edit their shell config.

-{"id":"br-28mgh.2.3","title":"T2.3: Atomic binary replacement","description":"Current install uses `install -m 0755 $SRC $DEST/am` which overwrites in-place.
If the system crashes mid-write, the binary is corrupted. Fix:

1. Write to temporary file: install -m 0755 $SRC $DEST/.am.tmp.$$
2. Sync to disk: sync (or fsync if available)
3. Atomic rename: mv $DEST/.am.tmp.$$ $DEST/am
4. Same for mcp-agent-mail binary
5. Clean up any stale .tmp files from previous failed installs

On Windows (if we add PowerShell installer), use Move-Item -Force which is atomic on NTFS.

 {"id":"br-28mgh.2.6","title":"T2.6: Windows PowerShell installer","description":"Create install.ps1 for Windows users. The bash installer cannot run on Windows
natively. PowerShell is the standard Windows scripting environment.

Scope:
1. Download Windows zip from GitHub releases
2. Extract to %LOCALAPPDATA%/Programs/mcp-agent-mail/ or user-specified dir
3. Add to User PATH (via [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable)
4. Verify installation
5. Handle sqlite3.dll bundling (must be next to .exe)
6. Support -Version, -Dest, -Force parameters
7. Support iwr (Invoke-WebRequest) pipe pattern:
   iwr -useb https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.ps1 | iex

Displacement means removing/renaming the Python am.exe in Scripts/ and ensuring the Rust am.exe is found first in PATH.","created_at":"2026-02-25T05:11:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-28mgh.2.7","title":"T2.7: Improve error messages with remediation","description":"Every error path in install.sh should include actionable remediation steps.
Audit all err() calls and ensure each one tells the user WHAT TO DO:

Current bad pattern:
  err "Binary not found in archive"

Better:
  err "Binary not found in archive"
  err ""
  err "This usually means the release tarball is malformed."
  err "Try:"
  err "  1. Re-run the installer: curl -fsSL ... | bash"
  err "  2. Use --force to skip version check"
  err "  3. Report at https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/mcp_agent_mail_rust/issues"

Also add a final catch-all trap that prints a help URL on any unexpected failure:
  trap 'err "Unexpected error. Report: https://github.com/..."; exit 1' ERR

 {"id":"br-28mgh.5","title":"T5: Storage and Config Migration","description":"Track 5: Storage and Config Migration

Both Python and Rust versions use the same storage root (~/.mcp_agent_mail_git_mailbox_repo)
and the same Git-backed archive format. Config uses .env files. This track ensures seamless
continuity of storage and configuration.

## Scope
- Verify Git archive compatibility (Python and Rust use same format)

Python: DATABASE_URL = "sqlite+aiosqlite:///./storage.sqlite3"

Rust: The database path is resolved relative to storage_root

 {"id":"br-28mgh.6","title":"T6: Self-Update Mechanism","description":"Track 6: Self-Update Mechanism

Users should be able to update to the latest version without re-running the installer.
This track implements `am self-update` (or `am update`) as a first-class CLI command.

## Scope
- Implement `am self-update` CLI command
- Check GitHub releases API for newer version
- Download and verify new binary (checksum + optional sigstore)
- Atomic binary replacement (write .tmp, rename)
- Handle in-use binary replacement (tricky on Windows)
- Print changelog/release notes summary
- Support --check flag (just check, do not update)
- Support --force flag (reinstall same version)
- Handle network errors gracefully
- Update both binaries (am + mcp-agent-mail)

## Design
Reuse existing install.sh logic where possible but from within Rust.
The self_update crate is already in Cargo.toml dependencies.
On Windows, the running binary cannot be replaced -- use a helper
script that waits for the process to exit then renames.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:32.348780717Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:32.348780717Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["track"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.6","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:32.348780717Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-28mgh.6.1","title":"T6.1: Check for updates via GitHub API","description":"Implement version check against GitHub releases API.

Flow:
1. GET https://api.github.com/repos/Dicklesworthstone/mcp_agent_mail_rust/releases/latest
2. Extract tag_name from response
3. Compare with current version (from built-in version string)
4. Return: UpdateAvailable { current, latest, url } | UpToDate | CheckFailed { reason }

Handle:

- Rate limiting (403) -> warn, CheckFailed
- No releases -> CheckFailed

CLI interface:

 {"id":"br-28mgh.6.3","title":"T6.3: Atomic in-place binary replacement","description":"Replace the running binary with the new version atomically.

Challenge: On Unix, a running binary can be replaced while executing
(the OS keeps the old inode alive until the process exits). On Windows,
the running binary is locked and cannot be replaced.

Unix strategy:
1. Write new binary to $DEST/.am.new.tmp
2. chmod 755
3. Rename old binary to $DEST/.am.old.tmp (backup)
4. Rename new binary to $DEST/am (atomic)
5. Same for mcp-agent-mail
6. Clean up .old.tmp files
7. Print "Update complete. Restart am to use the new version."

Windows strategy:
1. Write new binary to %DEST%/am.new.exe
2. Create update.bat script that:
   a. Waits for current process to exit (tasklist loop)
   b. Renames am.exe to am.old.exe  
   c. Renames am.new.exe to am.exe
   d. Deletes am.old.exe
   e. Deletes itself
3. Launch update.bat detached and exit

 {"id":"br-28mgh.7","title":"T7: Doctor Command Enhancement","description":"Track 7: Doctor Command Enhancement

The `am doctor` command should comprehensively verify the installation state and detect
any issues, including Python remnants, PATH conflicts, MCP config problems, database
format issues, and more.

## Scope
- Check that `am` resolves to the Rust binary (not an alias or Python)
- Check PATH order (DEST comes before any Python paths)
- Check for residual Python am alias in shell rc files
- Check MCP configs point to Rust binary
- Check database format (i64 vs TEXT timestamps)
- Check storage root accessibility and Git repo health
- Check for stale Python processes
- Check binary version matches expected
- Check for available updates
- Print remediation steps for each issue found
- Exit code reflects health (0=healthy, 1=issues found)

## Design
Each check is a separate function returning (status, message, fix_hint).
Doctor runs all checks and prints a summary table.
With --fix flag, doctor can attempt automatic remediation.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:32.645195803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:32.645195803Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["track"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28mgh.7","depends_on_id":"br-28mgh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:16:32.645195803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+# T5.3: Migrate .env configuration from Python to Rust
+# Python .env may live in clone dir or storage root. Rust reads the same
+# env vars but DATABASE_URL format differs (no aiosqlite prefix).
+migrate_env_config() {
+  # Find Python .env file
+  local env_file=""
+  local candidates=()
+  [ "$PYTHON_CLONE_FOUND" -eq 1 ] && [ -n "$PYTHON_CLONE_PATH" ] && candidates+=("$PYTHON_CLONE_PATH/.env")
+  candidates+=(
+    "$HOME/mcp_agent_mail/.env"
+    "$HOME/mcp-agent-mail/.env"
+    "$HOME/.mcp_agent_mail/.env"
+  )
+
+  for f in "${candidates[@]}"; do
+    if [ -f "$f" ]; then
+      env_file="$f"
+      break
+    fi
+  done
+
+  if [ -z "$env_file" ]; then
+    return 0  # No .env found, nothing to migrate
+  fi
+
+  info "Found Python .env at: $env_file"
+
+  # Rust config location
+  local rust_config_dir="$HOME/.config/mcp-agent-mail"
+  local rust_env="$rust_config_dir/config.env"
+
+  # Don't overwrite if Rust config already exists
+  if [ -f "$rust_env" ]; then
+    info "Rust config already exists at $rust_env — preserving"
+    return 0
+  fi
+
+  mkdir -p "$rust_config_dir"
+
+  # Vars that are compatible between Python and Rust
+  local compat_vars="HTTP_HOST HTTP_PORT HTTP_PATH HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN STORAGE_ROOT DATABASE_URL TUI_ENABLED LLM_ENABLED LLM_DEFAULT_MODEL WORKTREES_ENABLED"
+  # Python-only vars to skip
+  local skip_pattern="^(SQLALCHEMY_|ALEMBIC_|UVICORN_|ASYNC_)"
+
+  local tmpfile="${rust_env}.tmp.$$"
+  {
+    echo "# Migrated from Python .env: $env_file"
+    echo "# Migration date: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
+    echo ""
+
+    while IFS= read -r line || [ -n "$line" ]; do
+      # Skip comments and empty lines
+      case "$line" in
+        \#*|"") echo "$line"; continue;;
+      esac
+
+      local key val
+      key="${line%%=*}"
+      val="${line#*=}"
+
+      # Skip Python-specific vars
+      if echo "$key" | grep -qE "$skip_pattern"; then
+        echo "# Skipped (Python-only): $line"
+        continue
+      fi
+
+      # Transform DATABASE_URL: strip aiosqlite prefix, resolve path
+      if [ "$key" = "DATABASE_URL" ]; then
+        # Strip sqlite+aiosqlite:/// prefix → just the file path
+        local db_path
+        db_path=$(echo "$val" | sed 's|^sqlite[+a-z]*:///||')
+        # If relative path, make it relative to storage root
+        case "$db_path" in
+          /*) echo "DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///$db_path";;
+          *)  echo "DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///$RUST_STORAGE_ROOT/$db_path";;
+        esac
+        continue
+      fi
+
+      # Pass through compatible vars as-is
+      echo "$line"
+    done < "$env_file"
+  } > "$tmpfile"
+
+  mv "$tmpfile" "$rust_env"
+  chmod 600 "$rust_env"  # Restrict access (may contain tokens)
+  ok "Migrated .env config to $rust_env"
+}
+
+# T2.3: Atomic binary installation (crash-safe)
+# Writes to a temp file, syncs, then renames atomically.
+# Cleans up stale tmp files from previous failed installs.
+atomic_install() {
+  local src="$1"
+  local dest="$2"
+  local tmp_dest="${dest}.tmp.$$"
+
+  # Clean up stale tmp files from previous failed installs
+  for stale in "${dest}".tmp.*; do
+    [ -f "$stale" ] && rm -f "$stale" 2>/dev/null
+  done
+
+  # Write to temp file
+  install -m 0755 "$src" "$tmp_dest"
+
+  # Sync to disk if available
+  sync "$tmp_dest" 2>/dev/null || sync 2>/dev/null || true
+
+  # Atomic rename
+  mv -f "$tmp_dest" "$dest"
+}
+

+verify_installation() {

+

+  if [ ! -x "$DEST/$BIN_SERVER" ]; then
+    warn "VERIFY: $DEST/$BIN_SERVER is missing or not executable"
+    issues=$((issues + 1))
+  fi
+  if [ ! -x "$DEST/$BIN_CLI" ]; then
+    warn "VERIFY: $DEST/$BIN_CLI is missing or not executable"
+    issues=$((issues + 1))
+  fi
+
+  # 2. Check version output
+  local version_out
+  version_out=$("$DEST/$BIN_CLI" --version 2>&1 || true)
+  if [ -z "$version_out" ]; then
+    warn "VERIFY: 'am --version' produced no output"
+    issues=$((issues + 1))
+  else
+    ok "VERIFY: $version_out"
+  fi
+
+  # 3. Check that 'am' resolves to the Rust binary in a login shell
+  local shell_name
+  shell_name=$(basename "${SHELL:-/bin/sh}")
+  local resolve_cmd
+  case "$shell_name" in
+    zsh)  resolve_cmd="zsh -l -c 'whence -p am 2>/dev/null || which am 2>/dev/null || echo NOT_FOUND'" ;;
+    bash) resolve_cmd="bash -l -c 'type -P am 2>/dev/null || which am 2>/dev/null || echo NOT_FOUND'" ;;
+    *)    resolve_cmd="sh -l -c 'which am 2>/dev/null || echo NOT_FOUND'" ;;
+  esac
+  local resolved_path
+  resolved_path=$(eval "$resolve_cmd" 2>/dev/null || echo "NOT_FOUND")
+
+  if [ "$resolved_path" = "NOT_FOUND" ] || [ -z "$resolved_path" ]; then
+    warn "VERIFY: 'am' not found in login shell PATH"
+    warn "  You may need to restart your shell or run: export PATH=\"$DEST:\$PATH\""
+    issues=$((issues + 1))

+      warn "  Alias: $alias_check"
